heading: 9.7.14.5.2.Matrix Fragments formma.m8n8k4with.f64floating point type
content:
A warp executing `mma.m8n8k4` with `.f64` floating point type will compute an MMA operation of
shape `.m8n8k4`.

Elements of the matrix are distributed across the threads in a warp so each thread of the warp holds
a fragment of the matrix.

* Multiplicand A:

  | .atype | Fragment | Elements (low to high) |
  | --- | --- | --- |
  | `.f64` | A vector expression containing a single `.f64` register, containing single `.f64` element from the matrix A. | a0 |

  The layout of the fragments held by different threads is shown in [Figure 53](#mma-884-a-f64).

  



[Figure: mma-884-A-f64]
MMA .m8n8k4 Fragment Layout for Matrix A with .f64 Type

MATRIX IDENTIFICATION:
- Instruction: MMA (Matrix Multiply-Accumulate)
- Shape: .m8n8k4 (M=8, N=8, K=4)
- Matrix: A
- Data type: .f64 (64-bit double precision floating point)

GRID STRUCTURE:
- Rows represent matrix rows (0 through 7)
- Columns represent column indices 0 through 3 (4 columns total, corresponding to K=4)
- Each cell contains one thread:register_element assignment
- Notation format: T{thread_id}:a{register_index}

FULL LAYOUT TABLE:
Row\Col | Col 0    | Col 1    | Col 2    | Col 3
--------|----------|----------|----------|----------
Row 0   | T0:a0    | T1:a0    | T2:a0    | T3:a0
Row 1   | T4:a0    | T5:a0    | T6:a0    | T7:a0
Row 2   | (arrow indicating continuation pattern from row 1 to row 7)
..      | (arrow indicating reverse/continuation)
Row 7   | T28:a0   | T29:a0   | T30:a0   | T31:a0

THREAD-TO-ELEMENT MAPPING (explicit entries):
- T0:a0  occupies Row 0, Col 0
- T1:a0  occupies Row 0, Col 1
- T2:a0  occupies Row 0, Col 2
- T3:a0  occupies Row 0, Col 3
- T4:a0  occupies Row 1, Col 0
- T5:a0  occupies Row 1, Col 1
- T6:a0  occupies Row 1, Col 2
- T7:a0  occupies Row 1, Col 3
- T28:a0 occupies Row 7, Col 0
- T29:a0 occupies Row 7, Col 1
- T30:a0 occupies Row 7, Col 2
- T31:a0 occupies Row 7, Col 3

INFERRED PATTERN (rows 2 through 6, implied by arrows):
- Row 2: T8:a0, T9:a0, T10:a0, T11:a0
- Row 3: T12:a0, T13:a0, T14:a0, T15:a0
- Row 4: T16:a0, T17:a0, T18:a0, T19:a0
- Row 5: T20:a0, T21:a0, T22:a0, T23:a0
- Row 6: T24:a0, T25:a0, T26:a0, T27:a0

GROUPING PATTERN:
- Each group of 4 consecutive threads (T0-T3, T4-T7, ..., T28-T31) covers one full row of matrix A across all 4 K-columns.
- Each row of matrix A is assigned to exactly 4 threads.
- 8 rows total, 4 threads per row = 32 threads total (T0 through T31).

REGISTER PACKING:
- Each thread holds exactly one register fragment: a0
- Since the data type is .f64 (64-bit), each a0 fragment corresponds to one 64-bit double-precision value.
- Each thread holds one element of matrix A (one f64 value in register a0).
- No packing of multiple values into a single register (unlike f16 which packs two values per 32-bit register).

ADDRESSING NOTE:
- The figure label "%laneid:{fragments}" indicates that the lane ID (thread ID within the warp) directly determines which matrix element is held, following the pattern described above.

MMA .m8n8k4 Fragment Layout for Matrix A with .f64 Type

MATRIX IDENTIFICATION:
- Instruction: MMA (Matrix Multiply-Accumulate)
- Shape: .m8n8k4 (M=8, N=8, K=4)
- Matrix: A
- Data type: .f64 (64-bit double precision floating point)

GRID STRUCTURE:
- Rows represent matrix rows (0 through 7)
- Columns represent column indices 0 through 3 (4 columns total, corresponding to K=4)
- Each cell contains one thread:register_element assignment
- Notation format: T{thread_id}:a{register_index}

FULL LAYOUT TABLE:
Row\Col | Col 0    | Col 1    | Col 2    | Col 3
--------|----------|----------|----------|----------
Row 0   | T0:a0    | T1:a0    | T2:a0    | T3:a0
Row 1   | T4:a0    | T5:a0    | T6:a0    | T7:a0
Row 2   | (arrow indicating continuation pattern from row 1 to row 7)
..      | (arrow indicating reverse/continuation)
Row 7   | T28:a0   | T29:a0   | T30:a0   | T31:a0

THREAD-TO-ELEMENT MAPPING (explicit entries):
- T0:a0  occupies Row 0, Col 0
- T1:a0  occupies Row 0, Col 1
- T2:a0  occupies Row 0, Col 2
- T3:a0  occupies Row 0, Col 3
- T4:a0  occupies Row 1, Col 0
- T5:a0  occupies Row 1, Col 1
- T6:a0  occupies Row 1, Col 2
- T7:a0  occupies Row 1, Col 3
- T28:a0 occupies Row 7, Col 0
- T29:a0 occupies Row 7, Col 1
- T30:a0 occupies Row 7, Col 2
- T31:a0 occupies Row 7, Col 3

INFERRED PATTERN (rows 2 through 6, implied by arrows):
- Row 2: T8:a0, T9:a0, T10:a0, T11:a0
- Row 3: T12:a0, T13:a0, T14:a0, T15:a0
- Row 4: T16:a0, T17:a0, T18:a0, T19:a0
- Row 5: T20:a0, T21:a0, T22:a0, T23:a0
- Row 6: T24:a0, T25:a0, T26:a0, T27:a0

GROUPING PATTERN:
- Each group of 4 consecutive threads (T0-T3, T4-T7, ..., T28-T31) covers one full row of matrix A across all 4 K-columns.
- Each row of matrix A is assigned to exactly 4 threads.
- 8 rows total, 4 threads per row = 32 threads total (T0 through T31).

REGISTER PACKING:
- Each thread holds exactly one register fragment: a0
- Since the data type is .f64 (64-bit), each a0 fragment corresponds to one 64-bit double-precision value.
- Each thread holds one element of matrix A (one f64 value in register a0).
- No packing of multiple values into a single register (unlike f16 which packs two values per 32-bit register).

ADDRESSING NOTE:
- The figure label "%laneid:{fragments}" indicates that the lane ID (thread ID within the warp) directly determines which matrix element is held, following the pattern described above.


  Figure 53 MMA .m8n8k4 fragment layout for matrix A with `.f64` type

  The row and column of a matrix fragment can be computed as:

  ```
  row =        %laneid >> 2

  col =        %laneid % 4
  ```
* Multiplicand B:

  | .btype | Fragment | Elements (low to high) |
  | --- | --- | --- |
  | `.f64` | A vector expression containing a single `.f64` register, containing a single `.f64` element from the matrix B. | b0 |

  The layout of the fragments held by different threads is shown in [Figure 54](#mma-884-b-f64).

  



[Figure: mma-884-B-f64]
MMA .m8n8k4 Fragment Layout for Matrix B with .f64 Type

1. MATRIX DIMENSIONS AND IDENTITY
   Operation: MMA .m8n8k4
   Matrix: B (input matrix)
   Data type: .f64 (64-bit double precision floating point)

2. GRID STRUCTURE
   Rows: Matrix rows 0 through 3 (K dimension = 4, so rows 0-3)
   Columns: Matrix columns 0 through 7 (N dimension = 8, so columns 0-7)
   The table header shows columns: 0, 1, 2, .. (implied 3-6), 7

3. THREAD-TO-ELEMENT MAPPING (complete layout)

   Row\Column  |  Col 0   |  Col 1   |  Col 2  |  ...  |  Col 7
   ------------|----------|----------|---------|-------|--------
   Row 0       |  T0:b0   |  T4:b0   |  (cont) |  ...  |  T28:b0
   Row 1       |  T1:b0   |  T5:b0   |  (cont) |  ...  |  T29:b0
   Row 2       |  T2:b0   |  T6:b0   |  (cont) |  ...  |  T30:b0
   Row 3       |  T3:b0   |  T7:b0   |  (cont) |  ...  |  T31:b0

4. DETAILED THREAD ASSIGNMENTS
   Column 0: T0:b0 (row 0), T1:b0 (row 1), T2:b0 (row 2), T3:b0 (row 3)
   Column 1: T4:b0 (row 0), T5:b0 (row 1), T6:b0 (row 2), T7:b0 (row 3)
   Column 2: T8:b0 (row 0), T9:b0 (row 1), T10:b0 (row 2), T11:b0 (row 3)  [implied by pattern]
   Column 3: T12:b0 (row 0), T13:b0 (row 1), T14:b0 (row 2), T15:b0 (row 3) [implied]
   Column 4: T16:b0 (row 0), T17:b0 (row 1), T18:b0 (row 2), T19:b0 (row 3) [implied]
   Column 5: T20:b0 (row 0), T21:b0 (row 1), T22:b0 (row 2), T23:b0 (row 3) [implied]
   Column 6: T24:b0 (row 0), T25:b0 (row 1), T26:b0 (row 2), T27:b0 (row 3) [implied]
   Column 7: T28:b0 (row 0), T29:b0 (row 1), T30:b0 (row 2), T31:b0 (row 3)

5. GROUPING PATTERN
   Each group of 4 consecutive threads (T0-T3, T4-T7, T8-T11, ..., T28-T31) covers all 4 rows of a single column.
   Each group of 4 threads maps to one column of matrix B.
   There are 8 groups of 4 threads total, covering all 8 columns (N=8).
   Thread index within group determines the row (0-3).
   Group number (thread_id / 4) determines the column (0-7).

6. REGISTER PACKING
   Each thread holds exactly one fragment register: b0
   Since the data type is .f64 (64-bit), each b0 register holds one 64-bit double-precision value.
   No packing of multiple values per register (unlike f16 which packs two values).
   Each thread contributes one element of matrix B.

7. ADDRESSING FORMULA
   %laneid (lane ID within warp) maps as follows:
   column = laneid / 4  (integer division)
   row    = laneid % 4
   Register fragment: b0

MMA .m8n8k4 Fragment Layout for Matrix B with .f64 Type

1. MATRIX DIMENSIONS AND IDENTITY
   Operation: MMA .m8n8k4
   Matrix: B (input matrix)
   Data type: .f64 (64-bit double precision floating point)

2. GRID STRUCTURE
   Rows: Matrix rows 0 through 3 (K dimension = 4, so rows 0-3)
   Columns: Matrix columns 0 through 7 (N dimension = 8, so columns 0-7)
   The table header shows columns: 0, 1, 2, .. (implied 3-6), 7

3. THREAD-TO-ELEMENT MAPPING (complete layout)

   Row\Column  |  Col 0   |  Col 1   |  Col 2  |  ...  |  Col 7
   ------------|----------|----------|---------|-------|--------
   Row 0       |  T0:b0   |  T4:b0   |  (cont) |  ...  |  T28:b0
   Row 1       |  T1:b0   |  T5:b0   |  (cont) |  ...  |  T29:b0
   Row 2       |  T2:b0   |  T6:b0   |  (cont) |  ...  |  T30:b0
   Row 3       |  T3:b0   |  T7:b0   |  (cont) |  ...  |  T31:b0

4. DETAILED THREAD ASSIGNMENTS
   Column 0: T0:b0 (row 0), T1:b0 (row 1), T2:b0 (row 2), T3:b0 (row 3)
   Column 1: T4:b0 (row 0), T5:b0 (row 1), T6:b0 (row 2), T7:b0 (row 3)
   Column 2: T8:b0 (row 0), T9:b0 (row 1), T10:b0 (row 2), T11:b0 (row 3)  [implied by pattern]
   Column 3: T12:b0 (row 0), T13:b0 (row 1), T14:b0 (row 2), T15:b0 (row 3) [implied]
   Column 4: T16:b0 (row 0), T17:b0 (row 1), T18:b0 (row 2), T19:b0 (row 3) [implied]
   Column 5: T20:b0 (row 0), T21:b0 (row 1), T22:b0 (row 2), T23:b0 (row 3) [implied]
   Column 6: T24:b0 (row 0), T25:b0 (row 1), T26:b0 (row 2), T27:b0 (row 3) [implied]
   Column 7: T28:b0 (row 0), T29:b0 (row 1), T30:b0 (row 2), T31:b0 (row 3)

5. GROUPING PATTERN
   Each group of 4 consecutive threads (T0-T3, T4-T7, T8-T11, ..., T28-T31) covers all 4 rows of a single column.
   Each group of 4 threads maps to one column of matrix B.
   There are 8 groups of 4 threads total, covering all 8 columns (N=8).
   Thread index within group determines the row (0-3).
   Group number (thread_id / 4) determines the column (0-7).

6. REGISTER PACKING
   Each thread holds exactly one fragment register: b0
   Since the data type is .f64 (64-bit), each b0 register holds one 64-bit double-precision value.
   No packing of multiple values per register (unlike f16 which packs two values).
   Each thread contributes one element of matrix B.

7. ADDRESSING FORMULA
   %laneid (lane ID within warp) maps as follows:
   column = laneid / 4  (integer division)
   row    = laneid % 4
   Register fragment: b0


  Figure 54 MMA .m8n8k4 fragment layout for matrix B with `.f64` type

  The row and column of a matrix fragment can be computed as:

  ```
  row =        %laneid % 4

  col =        %laneid >> 2
  ```
* Accumulators (C or D):

  | .ctype / .dtype | Fragment | Elements (low to high) |
  | --- | --- | --- |
  | `.f64` | A vector expression containing of two `.f64` registers containing two `.f64` elements from the matrix C. | c0, c1 |

  The layout of the fragments held by different threads is shown in [Figure 55](#mma-884-c-f64).

  



[Figure: mma-884-C-f64]
MMA Fragment Layout Description: .m8n8k4 Accumulator Matrix C/D with .f64 Type

1. MATRIX DIMENSIONS AND TYPE
   Operation: mma .m8n8k4
   Matrix: C/D (accumulator)
   Data type: .f64 (64-bit double precision floating point)
   Matrix logical dimensions: 8 rows x 8 columns

2. GRID STRUCTURE
   Rows in figure: matrix rows 0 through 7 (M dimension = 8)
   Columns in figure: matrix columns 0 through 7 (N dimension = 8), shown as paired columns: {0,1}, {2,3}, {4,5}, {6,7}
   Total threads involved: T0 through T31 (32 threads)

3. COLUMN PAIRING
   Columns are grouped into 4 pairs:
   Pair 1: columns 0 and 1
   Pair 2: columns 2 and 3
   Pair 3: columns 4 and 5
   Pair 4: columns 6 and 7

4. THREAD-TO-ELEMENT MAPPING (per matrix row and column pair)

   Row 0:
     Columns 0-1: T0:  {c0, c1}
     Columns 2-3: T1:  {c0, c1}
     Columns 4-5: T2:  {c0, c1}
     Columns 6-7: T3:  {c0, c1}

   Row 1:
     Columns 0-1: T4:  {c0, c1}
     Columns 2-3: T5:  {c0, c1}
     Columns 4-5: T6:  {c0, c1}
     Columns 6-7: T7:  {c0, c1}

   Row 2:
     (implied continuation, shown by arrow indicating pattern continues)

   Rows 3-6:
     (denoted by ".." with bidirectional arrows indicating the pattern continues in the same manner)

   Row 7:
     Columns 0-1: T28: {c0, c1}
     Columns 2-3: T29: {c0, c1}
     Columns 4-5: T30: {c0, c1}
     Columns 6-7: T31: {c0, c1}

5. GROUPING PATTERN
   Each row of the 8x8 matrix is handled by 4 threads.
   Each group of 4 consecutive threads (T0-T3, T4-T7, ..., T28-T31) covers one full matrix row across all 8 columns.
   Thread assignment per row:
     Row 0: T0, T1, T2, T3
     Row 1: T4, T5, T6, T7
     Row 2: T8, T9, T10, T11   (implied)
     Row 3: T12, T13, T14, T15 (implied)
     Row 4: T16, T17, T18, T19 (implied)
     Row 5: T20, T21, T22, T23 (implied)
     Row 6: T24, T25, T26, T27 (implied)
     Row 7: T28, T29, T30, T31
   Within each row, each thread covers 2 consecutive columns:
     Thread offset 0 within group: columns 0-1
     Thread offset 1 within group: columns 2-3
     Thread offset 2 within group: columns 4-5
     Thread offset 3 within group: columns 6-7

6. REGISTER ELEMENT PACKING
   Each thread holds 2 elements: c0 and c1.
   Since the data type is .f64 (64-bit), each element (c0, c1) occupies one 64-bit register (or a pair of 32-bit registers as used in PTX for f64).
   c0 corresponds to the first column of the pair assigned to that thread.
   c1 corresponds to the second column of the pair assigned to that thread.
   Therefore each thread holds exactly 2 f64 accumulator values covering a 1-row by 2-column sub-tile of the 8x8 output matrix.

7. LABEL NOTE
   The figure footer reads: %laneid:{fragments}
   This indicates the mapping is expressed as lane ID to fragment registers, where each lane (thread) within the warp holds the listed fragment registers {c0, c1}.

MMA Fragment Layout Description: .m8n8k4 Accumulator Matrix C/D with .f64 Type

1. MATRIX DIMENSIONS AND TYPE
   Operation: mma .m8n8k4
   Matrix: C/D (accumulator)
   Data type: .f64 (64-bit double precision floating point)
   Matrix logical dimensions: 8 rows x 8 columns

2. GRID STRUCTURE
   Rows in figure: matrix rows 0 through 7 (M dimension = 8)
   Columns in figure: matrix columns 0 through 7 (N dimension = 8), shown as paired columns: {0,1}, {2,3}, {4,5}, {6,7}
   Total threads involved: T0 through T31 (32 threads)

3. COLUMN PAIRING
   Columns are grouped into 4 pairs:
   Pair 1: columns 0 and 1
   Pair 2: columns 2 and 3
   Pair 3: columns 4 and 5
   Pair 4: columns 6 and 7

4. THREAD-TO-ELEMENT MAPPING (per matrix row and column pair)

   Row 0:
     Columns 0-1: T0:  {c0, c1}
     Columns 2-3: T1:  {c0, c1}
     Columns 4-5: T2:  {c0, c1}
     Columns 6-7: T3:  {c0, c1}

   Row 1:
     Columns 0-1: T4:  {c0, c1}
     Columns 2-3: T5:  {c0, c1}
     Columns 4-5: T6:  {c0, c1}
     Columns 6-7: T7:  {c0, c1}

   Row 2:
     (implied continuation, shown by arrow indicating pattern continues)

   Rows 3-6:
     (denoted by ".." with bidirectional arrows indicating the pattern continues in the same manner)

   Row 7:
     Columns 0-1: T28: {c0, c1}
     Columns 2-3: T29: {c0, c1}
     Columns 4-5: T30: {c0, c1}
     Columns 6-7: T31: {c0, c1}

5. GROUPING PATTERN
   Each row of the 8x8 matrix is handled by 4 threads.
   Each group of 4 consecutive threads (T0-T3, T4-T7, ..., T28-T31) covers one full matrix row across all 8 columns.
   Thread assignment per row:
     Row 0: T0, T1, T2, T3
     Row 1: T4, T5, T6, T7
     Row 2: T8, T9, T10, T11   (implied)
     Row 3: T12, T13, T14, T15 (implied)
     Row 4: T16, T17, T18, T19 (implied)
     Row 5: T20, T21, T22, T23 (implied)
     Row 6: T24, T25, T26, T27 (implied)
     Row 7: T28, T29, T30, T31
   Within each row, each thread covers 2 consecutive columns:
     Thread offset 0 within group: columns 0-1
     Thread offset 1 within group: columns 2-3
     Thread offset 2 within group: columns 4-5
     Thread offset 3 within group: columns 6-7

6. REGISTER ELEMENT PACKING
   Each thread holds 2 elements: c0 and c1.
   Since the data type is .f64 (64-bit), each element (c0, c1) occupies one 64-bit register (or a pair of 32-bit registers as used in PTX for f64).
   c0 corresponds to the first column of the pair assigned to that thread.
   c1 corresponds to the second column of the pair assigned to that thread.
   Therefore each thread holds exactly 2 f64 accumulator values covering a 1-row by 2-column sub-tile of the 8x8 output matrix.

7. LABEL NOTE
   The figure footer reads: %laneid:{fragments}
   This indicates the mapping is expressed as lane ID to fragment registers, where each lane (thread) within the warp holds the listed fragment registers {c0, c1}.


  Figure 55 MMA .m8n8k4 fragment layout for accumulator matrix C/D with `.f64` type

  The row and column of a matrix fragment can be computed as:

  ```
  groupID           = %laneid >> 2
  threadID_in_group = %laneid % 4

  row =      groupID

  col =      (threadID_in_group * 2) + (i & 0x1)       for ci   where i = {0, 1}
  ```
